title: Zelda-themed Gamified Multiplication App for 4th Graders

requirements.summary: Design and apply a Zelda-inspired visual theme throughout the entire app
acceptanceCriteria: Primary background uses parchment/tan (#F2E8C9) or dark green tones consistent with Zelda palette
Buttons and interactive elements use golden yellow (#C9A84C) styling
Fantasy-themed typography applied to headings and level titles
Decorative border/frame elements on panels and cards resemble Zelda HUD style
Heart or life icons visible in the UI
Color scheme is consistent on all screens

file_operations.description: Add Zelda-inspired color palette to the theme configuration: dark forest green (#2D5A27) as primary, golden yellow (#C9A84C) for highlights, parchment/tan (#F2E8C9) for backgrounds, deep brown (#3B1F0A) for borders/text, and red (#8B0000) for danger. Configure fantasy serif and pixel-style font families.

Import fantasy fonts (e.g., Google Fonts fantasy serif or pixel fonts) and define global CSS variables for Zelda color palette. Add base styling for Zelda-themed decorative borders, panel frames, and HUD-like container classes. Set default body background to parchment tone.

Create a reusable Zelda-styled panel component with decorative borders, parchment background, and HUD-like frame styling. This component wraps content throughout the app to maintain consistent theming. Reference the generated parchment background image at frontend/public/assets/generated/parchment-bg.dim_800x600.png as a background texture.

Create a component that displays heart icons representing player lives/health. Accept props for total hearts and filled hearts. Use Zelda red (#8B0000) for hearts and styling consistent with the theme.

requirements.summary: Create a Home/Start screen with Zelda-styled title, description, and level unlock indicators
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen displays the app title 'Quest of Numbers' in fantasy-style heading
Three level indicators are shown with locked/unlocked visual states
'Begin Quest' button navigates to Level 1
Screen is styled with Zelda color palette

file_operations.description: Create the Home/Start screen component displaying the game title 'Quest of Numbers', a brief description ('Prove your multiplication mastery across 3 epic levels!'), and a 'Begin Quest' button that navigates to Level 1. Show three level indicators with locked/unlocked status using sword icons for unlocked levels and lock icons for locked levels. Fetch unlock state from backend using getUnlockedLevels(). Reference the generated title banner image at frontend/public/assets/generated/title-banner.dim_800x200.png for the title area and the triforce icon at frontend/public/assets/generated/triforce-icon.dim_128x128.png as an accent. Use the ZeldaPanel component for layout.

Set up React Router with routes for Home (/), Level 1 (/level/1), Level 2 (/level/2), Level 3 (/level/3), Level Complete (/level-complete), and Level Failed (/level-failed). Import and register all page components. Set Home as the default route.

requirements.summary: Implement 3 progressive multiplication levels with increasing difficulty appropriate for 4th graders
acceptanceCriteria: Level 1 generates random single-digit × single-digit multiplication questions
Level 2 generates random questions with at least one factor up to 12
Level 3 generates random questions involving a two-digit number multiplied by a single digit
Each level consists of exactly 10 questions
Player must score at least 8/10 to pass the level
Completing a level unlocks the next level
Questions are randomly generated each attempt

file_operations.description: Create utility functions to generate random multiplication questions for each level. Level 1: single-digit × single-digit (1-9). Level 2: one factor up to 12 (e.g., 1-12 × 1-9). Level 3: two-digit number × single digit (10-20 × 1-9). Each function returns an array of 10 question objects containing factors and the correct answer. Ensure randomness for each attempt.

Create a custom hook to manage game state including current question index, score, mistakes, lives (3 hearts), questions array, and pass/fail status. Include functions to submit an answer, calculate score, determine if the level is passed (≥8/10), and reset the game state. Track mistakes to manage heart loss (lose a heart after the 2nd mistake).

Create a Level page component that accepts level number as a route parameter. On mount, generate 10 questions using the questionGenerator utility for the appropriate level difficulty. Use the useGameState hook to manage gameplay. When a level is completed with score ≥8/10, call backend unlockNextLevel() and navigate to Level Complete. If score <8/10, navigate to Level Failed. Pass level number and score to result pages via navigation state.

requirements.summary: Build a gameplay screen for each level showing level name, question counter, hearts, question, input, submit button, and feedback
acceptanceCriteria: Level name and banner displayed at top of gameplay screen
Question counter (e.g., 'Question X of 10') is visible
Heart icons displayed representing player lives/health
Multiplication question is displayed prominently in large text
Numeric input field accepts player's answer
Submit button triggers answer evaluation
Correct answer triggers green visual feedback with 'Correct!' text
Wrong answer triggers red visual feedback with 'Wrong! The answer was X' text
Progress bar reflects answered question count

file_operations.description: Create a GameplayUI component that renders the complete gameplay interface. Display level name with Zelda-style title banner at the top, question counter (e.g., 'Question 3 of 10'), HeartIcons component showing current lives, the multiplication question in large fantasy-styled text, a numeric input field, and a golden yellow 'Submit' button. Include a progress bar showing questions answered out of 10. Use ZeldaPanel for layout. Accept props for level number, current question, question index, total questions, lives, and submit handler.

Create a FeedbackMessage component that displays immediate visual feedback after answer submission. Show a green glow effect with 'Correct!' message for correct answers. Show a red flash effect with 'Wrong! The answer was X' message (displaying the correct answer) for incorrect answers. Use Zelda color palette for styling. Accept props for feedback type (correct/wrong), visibility state, and correct answer.

Integrate GameplayUI and FeedbackMessage components into the Level page. Pass game state from useGameState hook to GameplayUI. Handle answer submission by evaluating the answer, updating game state, showing feedback for 1-2 seconds, then advancing to the next question or completing the level. Display FeedbackMessage when feedback is active.

requirements.summary: Implement Level Complete and Level Failed screens with Zelda-styled messages and navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Level Complete screen shows victory message with Zelda-themed flavor text
Level Complete screen shows player score out of 10
'Continue to Next Level' or 'Return to Map' button is functional
Level Failed screen shows defeat message with Zelda-themed flavor text
Level Failed screen shows player score
'Try Again' button restarts the current level with new random questions

file_operations.description: Create LevelComplete page component shown after passing a level (score ≥8/10). Display a Zelda-styled victory message (e.g., 'You obtained the Forest Medallion!' for Level 1, 'The Temple is yours!' for Level 2, 'The Final Dungeon is conquered!' for Level 3). Show the player's score out of 10. Provide a 'Continue to Next Level' button that navigates to the next level (or 'Return to Map' button if on Level 3, navigating back to Home). Use ZeldaPanel and Zelda color styling. Accept level number and score via navigation state or route params.

Create LevelFailed page component shown when score <8/10. Display a Zelda-styled defeat message (e.g., 'The dungeon defeated you...'). Show the player's score out of 10. Provide a 'Try Again' button that navigates back to the same level to restart with new random questions. Use ZeldaPanel and Zelda color styling. Accept level number and score via navigation state or route params.
Register routes for LevelComplete (/level-complete) and LevelFailed (/level-failed) pages in the React Router configuration. Ensure navigation state can pass level number and score between routes.

requirements.summary: Persist level unlock progress in the backend and sync with frontend
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores unlock state for levels 1, 2, and 3
Level 1 is always unlocked by default
Level 2 unlocks only after Level 1 is passed
Level 3 unlocks only after Level 2 is passed
Frontend reads unlock state from backend on app load
Frontend updates backend when a level is passed successfully
Locked levels are visually indicated as inaccessible on the home screen

file_operations.description: Create a custom hook that manages level unlock state by interacting with the backend. Fetch unlock state using getUnlockedLevels() on mount and store it in React state. Provide a function to unlock the next level by calling backend unlockNextLevel(level) when a level is passed. Return unlock state and unlock function for use in components.

Integrate useLevelUnlock hook into the Home component. Display level indicators with locked/unlocked visual states based on the unlock state from the backend. Disable navigation to locked levels. Show sword icons for unlocked levels and lock icons for locked levels. Ensure Level 1 is always accessible.
After successfully completing a level with score ≥8/10, call the unlock function from useLevelUnlock hook to unlock the next level in the backend before navigating to LevelComplete page.

requirements.summary: Wire generated static image assets into the app UI
acceptanceCriteria: Parchment scroll background texture image is rendered in panel/card backgrounds
Triforce-inspired golden icon is rendered as an accent on the home screen
Game logo banner image is displayed on the home screen title area
All images are served from frontend/public/assets/generated

file_operations.description: Update ZeldaPanel component to use the generated parchment background texture at frontend/public/assets/generated/parchment-bg.dim_800x600.png as a repeating or contained background image for all panel instances throughout the app.

Update Home page to display the generated title banner image at frontend/public/assets/generated/title-banner.dim_800x200.png in the title area. Render the triforce icon at frontend/public/assets/generated/triforce-icon.dim_128x128.png as a decorative accent near the title or level indicators.
